questions.question: In the ultimatum game, what happens when one player rejects the proposed division of the payoff?
options: The rejected player leaves the game.
The rejected player accepts the offer.
The game ends in a tie.
The rejected player starts a new round.
questions.explanation: When a player rejects the proposed division of the payoff, they choose to leave the game rather than accept the offered amount.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key characteristic of the ultimatum game?
options: It is a game of chance.
It is a game of skill.
It is a game where both players have the same payoff.
It is a game where one player proposes a division of the payoff and the other player can either accept or reject.
questions.explanation: The ultimatum game is characterized by one player proposing a division of the payoff, and the other player having the option to accept or reject the offer.

questions.question: What is the expected outcome in the ultimatum game when both players play optimally?
options: The players both receive a high payoff.
The players both receive a low payoff.
One player receives a high payoff and the other player receives a low payoff.
The game ends in a tie.
questions.explanation: When both players play optimally, they both receive a low payoff, as the optimal strategy is to reject the proposed division and walk away with nothing.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a Nash equilibrium in the ultimatum game?
options: Player 1 proposes a division of 100/0.
Player 1 proposes a division of 50/50.
Player 1 proposes a division of 0/100.
Player 2 accepts any proposed division.
questions.explanation: A Nash equilibrium is reached when both players choose the best response to the other player's action, which in this case is when Player 1 proposes a division of 100/0 and Player 2 accepts it.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the ultimatum game in game theory?
options: To test the fairness of proposed divisions.
To study the behavior of players in situations where one player has more power.
To analyze the impact of cooperation on the outcome of a game.
To investigate the role of communication in games.
questions.explanation: The ultimatum game is used to study the behavior of players in situations where one player has more power, such as in situations where one player has a greater share of the payoff.
